title: RFC-ATF-6: Agent Trust Fabric — Behavioral Execution Verification Protocol. Behavioral Anchor Record, Constraint Conformance Signal, and Cross-Turn Coherence Hash Chain
description:
RFC-ATF-6 specifies the Behavioral Execution Verification Layer (BEV) of the Agent Trust Fabric — the sixth RFC in the ATF Open Standard series published by OMNIX QUANTUM LTD.

RFC-ATF-6 answers the question no prior RFC addresses: did the agent actually behave within its authorized constraints during execution, turn by turn?

Three protocol components are introduced:

  • Behavioral Anchor Record (BAR): A PQC-signed record produced at each execution turn, binding the SHA3-256 hash of the agent's output to the governing receipt and turn index via content_hash = SHA3-256(output_hash ‖ receipt_id ‖ turn_index). The BAR is the first ATF artifact that closes the authorization-to-output chain: every authorized action has a verifiable, tamper-evident record of what the agent actually produced. A HALTED BAR immediately stops the session. Sealed with Dilithium-3 (ML-DSA-65, FIPS 204). Six invariants: BEV-INV-001 through BEV-INV-004, BEV-INV-015, BEV-INV-016.
  • Constraint Conformance Signal (CCS): A conformance_score in [0.0, 1.0] computed atomically with each BAR, measuring behavioral conformance against the constraint vector in the governing receipt. Drift = 1.0 - conformance_score per turn; cumulative drift triggers HALT when it exceeds the threshold (default 0.35). Verdicts: CONFORMANT / WARNING / CRITICAL / HALT. CRITICAL triggers AGVP PVR issuance. HALT blocks all subsequent turns. Drift accumulator isolated per session_id and reload-safe on process restart. Six invariants: BEV-INV-005 through BEV-INV-009, BEV-INV-017.
  • Cross-Turn Coherence Hash Chain (CTCHC): A hash chain where every link = SHA3-256(json.dumps({"prev": prev_hash, "turn": output_hash, "receipt": receipt_id}, sort_keys=True)), seeded by a genesis hash bound to session_id and governing_receipt_id. The chain seal (ML-DSA-65) is the Session Integrity Proof: offline-verifiable detection of turn omission, turn reordering, and cross-session BAR substitution. Every link's receipt_id must match the chain receipt. Six invariants: BEV-INV-010 through BEV-INV-014, BEV-INV-018.

Novel contributions:

  • Behavioral Anchor Record (BAR) — first PQC-signed, governance-receipt-bound per-turn behavioral output attestation artifact in any published governance specification
  • Constraint Conformance Signal (CCS) — first governance-native behavioral conformance metric referencing the governing receipt's constraint vector (not a separate monitoring policy)
  • Cross-Turn Coherence Hash Chain (CTCHC) — first receipt-seeded hash chain for multi-turn AI agent session behavioral coherence verification
  • CCS-AGVP integration loop — first mechanism connecting behavioral output monitoring to anticipatory governance veto issuance in a cryptographically attested protocol
  • Behavioral Attestation Chain — first end-to-end forensic artifact spanning authorization → per-turn behavioral output → conformance measurement → session coherence proof
  • ATF-BEV-Compliant — sixth compliance tier, 106 formally specified invariants across 20 protocol families

Formal verification: Five Z3 SMT and structural proof targets (BEV-FVS-001 through BEV-FVS-005) covering BAR binding, CCS score bounds [0.0, 100.0], CCS component non-negativity, and CTCHC chain monotonicity. Machine-reproducible via python omnix_core/formal_verification/run_bev_proofs.py.

Compliance: ATF-BEV-Compliant designation requires satisfaction of all RFC-ATF-1 through RFC-ATF-6 requirements — 106 invariants across 20 protocol families including BAR, CCS, and CTCHC operational with AGVP integration.

Regulatory alignment: EU AI Act Art. 9, 12, 13, 72 · NIST AI RMF MEASURE 2.6, MANAGE 2.2, GOVERN 1.7 · ISO/IEC 42001 §8.4, §9.1 · UK AISI §3-5 · MiFID II Art. 17 · GCC/DIFC · SOC 2 Type II CC7.2

ATF series: RFC-ATF-1 (Identity & Delegation, Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.20155016) · RFC-ATF-2 (Runtime Continuity, Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.20241344) · RFC-ATF-3 (Governance Policy Interoperability & Forensic Evidence, Zenodo 10.5281/zenodo.20247342) · RFC-ATF-4 (Proactive Governance Layer, pending) · RFC-ATF-5 (Cognitive Governance Layer, pending)

OMNIX QUANTUM LTD, registered in England and Wales. Harold Alberto Nunes Rodelo, Founder and Editor. Operational headquarters: Abu Dhabi, UAE.

notes: OMNIX QUANTUM LTD is registered in England and Wales (71-75 Shelton Street, Covent Garden, London WC2H 9JQ). Harold Alberto Nunes Rodelo is Founder and Editor. Operational headquarters: Abu Dhabi, UAE. Priority records: OMNIX-PAR-2026-BAR-001 · OMNIX-PAR-2026-CCS-001 · OMNIX-PAR-2026-CTCHC-001. RFC-ATF-6 is the sixth and final layer of the Agent Trust Fabric Open Standard series. Related ADRs: ADR-181 (BAR) · ADR-182 (CCS) · ADR-183 (CTCHC). This document is the normative specification. New database tables: atf_behavioral_anchor_records · atf_constraint_conformance_signals · atf_coherence_hash_chains. 18 BEV invariants: BAR (BEV-INV-001–004, 015–016), CCS (BEV-INV-005–009, 017), CTCHC (BEV-INV-010–014, 018). 106 total ATF invariants across 20 protocol families. ATF-BEV-Compliant is the sixth and highest compliance tier.

_submission_checklist.note: Before submitting, verify that RFC-ATF-4 and RFC-ATF-5 DOIs are in Published (not Draft) status on Zenodo. Do not include their DOIs in the references field above until verified.
submission_steps: 6. Copy DOI and update replit.md + RFC-ATF-6.md header
7. Update RFC-ATF-4 and RFC-ATF-5 references in RFC-ATF-6 once their DOIs are confirmed
